name: Popeye's Fried Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first nine ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Put the cornflakes into another bowl.
3. Mix the eggs and water in a separate bowl.
4. Heat enough corn oil in a heavy, roomy skillet to fill it about 1 inch deep. Get it very hot.
5. Grease a 9x12x2 baking pan.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
7. Dip chicken pieces one at a time:
- First, into the dry coating mix.
- Second, into the egg and water mixture.
- Third, into the crushed cornflakes.
- Fourth, quickly but briefly back into the dry mix.
8. Drop the coated chicken pieces into the hot oil, skin-side down. Brown for 3 to 4 minutes on medium-high heat. Turn and brown the other side as well.
9. Do not crowd the pieces during frying. Place them in the prepared pan in a single layer, skin-side up.
10. Cover the pan with foil, leaving one side loose for steam to escape. Bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes. Remove the foil and test for tenderness. Allow to bake uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to crisp the coating.
11. Serves 4. Leftovers refrigerate well up to 4 days. Do not freeze leftovers. The leftover coating mix (first nine ingredients) can be stored at room temperature in a covered container up to 2 months.
